description: Vault Commands - Create, manage, deposit, withdraw, and delete single-asset vaults on XRPL (devnet only — SingleAssetVault amendment not on testnet)

userStories.title: vault create command
userStories.description: As a CLI user, I want to create a single-asset vault so that I can pool assets and issue shares to depositors.
acceptanceCriteria: Implement src/commands/vault.ts with vaultCommand registered in src/commands/index.ts and src/index.ts
`xrpl vault create --asset <asset>` submits VaultCreate tx; --asset uses standard amount format to identify asset type (e.g. `0` for XRP, `0/USD/rIssuer` for IOU)
`--assets-maximum <n>` optional UInt64 string sets AssetsMaximum cap
`--mpt-metadata <hex>` optional MPTokenMetadata for vault shares (max 1024 bytes hex)
`--domain-id <hash>` optional 64-char hex DomainID for private vault
`--private` flag sets tfVaultPrivate; validation error if used without --domain-id
On success, output `Vault ID: <hash>` extracted from CreatedNode where LedgerEntryType === 'Vault' in tx metadata
`--dry-run` prints unsigned tx JSON and exits without submitting
Validation tests (no network): missing --asset exits with error, --private without --domain-id exits with error, invalid --domain-id format exits with error
E2E tests on devnet (wss://s.devnet.rippletest.net:51233): XRP vault creation, --assets-maximum, --json, --dry-run, --no-wait
userStories.notes: VaultCreate fee is elevated (0.2 XRP = 200000 drops) — set Fee explicitly or verify autofill covers it. Extract VaultID from CreatedNode.LedgerIndex where LedgerEntryType === 'Vault'. E2E tests MUST use devnet, not testnet.

userStories.title: vault set command
userStories.description: As a CLI user, I want to update a vault I own so that I can change its metadata, asset cap, or domain.
acceptanceCriteria: --vault-id required; validated as 64-char hex; error if missing or malformed
`--domain-id <hash>` updates the domain (required when updating a private vault)
At least one of --data, --assets-maximum, --domain-id must be provided; error otherwise
Validation tests (no network): missing --vault-id, invalid --vault-id, no update fields provided
E2E tests on devnet: update --data on a vault created in beforeAll, update --assets-maximum, --json, --dry-run
userStories.notes: VaultSet cannot change public/private status — that is immutable after creation. AssetsMaximum cannot be lowered below current holdings (ledger enforces, not CLI).

userStories.title: vault deposit command
userStories.description: As a CLI user, I want to deposit assets into a vault so that I receive vault shares in return.
acceptanceCriteria: --amount required; uses standard format (e.g. `10` for XRP, `10/USD/rIssuer` for IOU, `10/mpt_issuance_id` for MPT)
Validation tests (no network): missing --vault-id, missing --amount, invalid --vault-id format
E2E tests on devnet: XRP deposit into vault created in beforeAll, --json, --dry-run, --no-wait
userStories.notes: No transfer fees apply to VaultDeposit. For public vaults any funded account can deposit. Deposit test should create vault in beforeAll then deposit in each test case.

userStories.title: vault withdraw command
userStories.description: As a CLI user, I want to withdraw assets from a vault by redeeming my shares so that I can recover my deposited funds.
acceptanceCriteria: --amount required; can specify asset quantity (burns shares to deliver amount) or share quantity (redeems shares for assets) — both use standard amount format
`--destination <address>` optionally sends redeemed assets to a different account
`--destination-tag <n>` optional UInt32 tag; error if used without --destination
Validation tests (no network): missing --vault-id, missing --amount, --destination-tag without --destination
E2E tests on devnet: deposit then withdraw XRP, --destination, --json, --dry-run, --no-wait
userStories.notes: VaultWithdraw does not respect PermissionedDomain rules — any shareholder can withdraw regardless. No transfer fees apply.

userStories.title: vault delete command
userStories.description: As a CLI user, I want to delete an empty vault I own so that I can remove it from the ledger and reclaim the reserve.
acceptanceCriteria: --vault-id required; 64-char hex; error if missing or malformed
Validation tests (no network): missing --vault-id, invalid --vault-id format
E2E tests on devnet: create vault then delete it, verify gone via ledger_entry RPC, --json, --dry-run, --no-wait
userStories.notes: Vault must be completely empty before deletion — tecHAS_OBLIGATIONS if not. E2E delete test should use a fresh vault with no deposits.

userStories.title: vault clawback command
userStories.description: As a token issuer, I want to claw back assets from a vault holder so that I can recover funds when regulatory or compliance requirements demand it.
acceptanceCriteria: --holder required; valid r-address of account whose shares to claw back; error if missing or invalid format
`--amount <amount>` optional; if omitted, claws back all available funds; uses standard amount format
Help text explicitly notes VaultClawback cannot claw back XRP
Validation tests (no network): missing --vault-id, missing --holder, invalid --holder address format
E2E tests on devnet: IOU vault — issuer creates vault, holder deposits, issuer claws back; partial clawback with --amount; --json; --dry-run
userStories.notes: Only the token/MPT issuer can call VaultClawback — not for XRP vaults. The issuer must have enabled clawback on the asset. E2E setup: issuer creates IOU vault, holder deposits, issuer claws back holder's shares.
